stack.ecommerce_engine: Saleor Cloud (GraphQL API, webhooks, Saleor Apps, channels, product metadata)
stack.frontend: Next.js (App Router) on Vercel — SSR/ISR storefront, Polish locale
stack.cloud_provider: AWS, region eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) — closest region to Poland with in-region Amazon Bedrock and EU data residency
stack.ai_platform: Amazon Bedrock (EU cross-region inference profiles keep traffic within EU regions)

architecture_overview.summary: Saleor Cloud remains the single source of truth for catalog, pricing, availability, and partner status. A dedicated AI Search service on AWS indexes the catalog into Amazon OpenSearch (hybrid BM25 + vector) and orchestrates retrieval-augmented suggestion generation with Claude on Amazon Bedrock. The Next.js storefront on Vercel calls the AI Search API server-side; classic search and catalog browsing query OpenSearch directly through the same API layer. Admin curation lives in a Saleor App embedded in the Saleor Dashboard, backed by DynamoDB. Hallucination is structurally prevented: the LLM can only rank and explain products retrieved from the index — it never generates product data.
architecture_overview.diagram_ascii: Customer -> Next.js (Vercel) -> API Gateway + WAF (AWS eu-central-1) -> Lambda Orchestrator -> [Guardrails -> DynamoDB rules/exclusions -> OpenSearch hybrid retrieval -> Bedrock Claude Haiku 4.5 (EU profile)] ; Saleor Cloud -> webhooks -> EventBridge/SQS -> Lambda Indexer -> (Bedrock embeddings) -> OpenSearch ; Admin -> Saleor App (Dashboard) -> DynamoDB + Athena analytics

key_architecture_decisions.decision: RAG over fine-tuning: hybrid retrieval (BM25 + k-NN vector) from OpenSearch, then Claude Haiku 4.5 ranks the retrieved candidates and writes fit explanations
key_architecture_decisions.rationale: Catalog changes daily; retrieval grounds every suggestion in real, currently available products. The LLM receives only candidate product IDs + attributes and must answer with IDs from that set — fabricated products are impossible by construction (invalid IDs are dropped at validation).
alternatives_rejected: Pure vector search without LLM (no natural-language explanations, weaker intent understanding)
Bedrock Knowledge Bases (less control over Saleor-specific filtering: partner suspension, seasonal availability, exclusion lists)

key_architecture_decisions.decision: LLM: Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5 on Bedrock via EU cross-region inference profile eu.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5-20251001-v1:0
key_architecture_decisions.rationale: Available in eu-central-1 with EU-geo routing (GDPR-friendly, data stays in EU regions); strong Polish quality; low per-token cost and low latency fit a high-volume consumer search box. Verified via AWS docs: Claude Haiku 4.5 is available in Frankfurt in-region and through the EU geo profile.
alternatives_rejected: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (2-3x cost, unnecessary for rank-and-explain over <=30 candidates; keep as escalation option for quality issues)
Amazon Nova Lite (cheaper but weaker Polish fluency — re-evaluate after PoC benchmark)

key_architecture_decisions.decision: Embeddings: Cohere Embed Multilingual on Bedrock as primary candidate; Amazon Titan Text Embeddings V2 (1024 dims) as fallback — final pick decided by a Polish retrieval-quality spike
key_architecture_decisions.rationale: Polish is a first-class language in Cohere's multilingual embed models (100+ languages). Titan V2 lists multilingual support as preview, so it must be benchmarked before production use. Hybrid search with a Polish analyzer (stempel/morfologik) on the BM25 side compensates for embedding weaknesses either way.
key_architecture_decisions.verification_required: Confirm in-region availability of the chosen embedding model in eu-central-1 at implementation time; avoid the global.cohere-embed-v4.0 profile if EU-only data routing is required.

key_architecture_decisions.decision: One search engine for everything: Amazon OpenSearch serves AI vector retrieval, classic search (typo tolerance, search-as-you-type), and catalog facet counts
key_architecture_decisions.rationale: Single index, single sync pipeline, consistent availability filtering across AI and classic surfaces, and the classic search doubles as the always-on fallback. Start with a small provisioned Multi-AZ domain (predictable ~low-hundreds USD/month at MVP catalog size, supports the stempel Polish analysis plugin); OpenSearch Serverless (NextGen, scale-to-zero OCUs) is the zero-ops alternative if operational simplicity outweighs cost predictability.
alternatives_rejected: Aurora/RDS pgvector (weaker typo tolerance, no facet counts out of the box)
Algolia + separate vector DB (second vendor, second sync pipeline, higher TCO)
Saleor built-in search (insufficient for fuzzy matching, facets, and vectors)

key_architecture_decisions.decision: Moderation and abuse control: Bedrock Guardrails (Standard tier) + AWS WAF + per-visitor quotas + query cache
key_architecture_decisions.rationale: Guardrails Standard tier lists Polish as 'optimized and supported' for content filters and prompt-attack detection (requires cross-region inference, satisfied by the EU profile). WAF rate-limits by IP; DynamoDB counters enforce per-visitor daily limits; a normalized-query answer cache cuts repeat-query LLM cost significantly.

key_architecture_decisions.decision: Admin curation as a Saleor App embedded in the Saleor Dashboard
key_architecture_decisions.rationale: Administrators stay in one tool. The app (Next.js, deployed on Vercel) manages the AI exclusion list, manual rules, and the discovery analytics dashboard via a small internal API to DynamoDB and Athena. Saleor app tokens + dashboard SSO handle auth.

components.name: Storefront AI Search (Next.js on Vercel)
responsibilities: Homepage hero: natural-language input (Polish), submits to /api/ai-search route handler (server-side — no AWS credentials in browser)
Renders 3-5 suggestion cards: photo, name, location, price, rating, exclusivity badge, 1-2 sentence fit explanation, link to standard product page
'Show more suggestions' sends the session cursor to fetch the next distinct 3-5 matches
Graceful fallback: on AI API error/timeout (client budget 6 s), the query is passed to classic search with a friendly notice — no dead end
Classic search UI: debounced search-as-you-type suggestions, typo-tolerant results, filters (price, category, location, attributes) with per-filter result counts, sorting
components.tech: Next.js App Router, React Server Components, Vercel Edge Network; AI-search feature flag via environment/config for instant kill-switch

components.name: AI Search API (AWS)
responsibilities: API Gateway (HTTP API) fronted by AWS WAF: IP rate limiting, bot control, max payload size
Lambda Orchestrator (TypeScript): the AI query pipeline (see data_flows.ai_search_query)
Search Proxy Lambda: classic search, autocomplete, and faceted catalog queries against OpenSearch
Per-visitor quota check (anonymous visitor ID cookie -> DynamoDB atomic counters, e.g. 15 AI queries/day, configurable)
Normalized query cache (DynamoDB with TTL): identical recent queries return cached suggestions without invoking Bedrock
components.tech: API Gateway + WAF + Lambda + DynamoDB, all eu-central-1; infrastructure as code with AWS CDK

components.name: Search Index (Amazon OpenSearch)
responsibilities: Single product index: name, description, category path, partner, location, tags, price, rating, image URL, exclusivity flag, availability flags (partner_active, seasonally_available, published, in_stock), dense vector field (k-NN)
Polish text analysis (stempel plugin) + edge n-grams/completion suggester for search-as-you-type + fuzziness:AUTO for typo tolerance
Hybrid query: BM25 + k-NN with score normalization; hard filter clause always applied: partner_active AND seasonally_available AND published AND NOT excluded_from_ai (AI path only)
Aggregations power facet counts on catalog browsing
components.tech: OpenSearch provisioned domain, 3 data nodes Multi-AZ (MVP sizing; catalog of tens of thousands of products is small for OpenSearch)

components.name: Catalog Sync Pipeline (Saleor -> OpenSearch)
responsibilities: Saleor webhooks (PRODUCT_CREATED/UPDATED/DELETED, PRODUCT_VARIANT_*, channel/listing changes) -> API Gateway -> SQS (buffering, retry, DLQ) -> Indexer Lambda
Indexer fetches full product via Saleor GraphQL, builds the embedding text (name + description + category + tags + attributes), calls Bedrock embeddings, upserts the OpenSearch document

Partner suspension and seasonal availability read from Saleor channel listings / product metadata and stored as filterable booleans — suspended or unavailable products drop out of AI suggestions on the next sync event (target < 5 min)
Nightly full reconciliation (EventBridge Scheduler -> Step Functions): re-walks the Saleor catalog to heal missed webhooks; re-embeds only when content hash changed (cost control)
components.tech: SQS, Lambda, Step Functions, EventBridge Scheduler, Saleor GraphQL API + webhooks (registered by the Saleor App)

components.name: Suggestion Engine (Bedrock)
responsibilities: Guardrails (Standard tier, Polish-optimized) screen input for abuse/off-topic/prompt injection and screen output before it reaches the customer

Manual-rules matcher: admin rules ('for requests like X, suggest Y') are embedded; if cosine similarity of the query to a rule exceeds threshold, pinned products are injected at the top of the candidate set
Retrieval: top ~25 candidates from OpenSearch hybrid query (after hard filters); exclusive products get a retrieval-score boost

Claude Haiku 4.5 prompt: system rules (Polish output, honesty, favor exclusives only when genuinely fitting, never invent) + user query + candidate list (id, name, category, price, location, key attributes, exclusive flag); structured JSON output: chosen ids (3-5), per-product 1-2 sentence explanation, match_confidence

Post-validation: every returned id must exist in the candidate set, else dropped; low match_confidence or empty result triggers the honest 'not available' response with closest alternatives and logs an unmatched-demand event
'Show more': cursor stored in DynamoDB (TTL 30 min) tracks already-shown ids; next invocation excludes them from candidates
components.tech: Amazon Bedrock: eu.anthropic.claude-haiku-4-5 inference profile, Bedrock Guardrails Standard tier, embedding model per ADR-3

components.name: Admin Curation & Analytics (Saleor App)
responsibilities: Exclusion list: products the AI must never suggest (DynamoDB; also mirrored as excluded_from_ai flag in OpenSearch on save — takes effect immediately)
Manual rules CRUD: phrase/intent -> pinned products; stored with embeddings in DynamoDB
Dashboard: anonymized query log, suggestion click-through rate, unmatched requests (demand insights), AI usage volume and estimated cost
Anonymization: no customer identifiers stored; visitor ID hashed with rotating salt; queries retained 12 months for demand analysis
components.tech: Saleor App (embedded Next.js on Vercel) -> internal API (API Gateway + Lambda, IAM auth) -> DynamoDB; events stream via Kinesis Data Firehose -> S3 -> Athena for the dashboard queries

components.name: Observability & Cost Control
responsibilities: CloudWatch dashboards: p95 latency per pipeline stage, Bedrock token consumption, cache hit rate, quota rejections, fallback activations
Alarms: error-rate and latency SLO breaches, daily Bedrock spend threshold, AWS Budgets alert at 80% of monthly AI budget
Kill switch: feature flag disables AI search storefront-wide within minutes, storefront silently degrades to classic search
Offline quality harness: curated set of ~200 Polish test queries with expected products; run before every prompt/model/index change (LLM-as-judge + human spot check)
components.tech: CloudWatch, X-Ray, AWS Budgets, Bedrock invocation logging (to S3, EU-only)

steps: 1. Customer submits Polish free-text query in homepage hero
2. Next.js route handler -> API Gateway (WAF: IP rate limit) -> Orchestrator Lambda
3. Quota check (DynamoDB) -> reject politely with classic-search redirect if exceeded
4. Cache check (normalized query) -> return cached suggestions on hit
5. Guardrails input screen -> blocked queries get a safe refusal in Polish
6. Manual-rule semantic match -> pinned products collected
7. Query embedded -> OpenSearch hybrid retrieval (top ~25, hard availability/exclusion filters, exclusive boost)
8. Claude Haiku 4.5 ranks candidates, returns 3-5 ids + explanations as JSON
9. Post-validation against candidate set; Guardrails output screen
10. Zero-fit path: honest 'not in our offer' + closest alternatives + unmatched-demand event to Firehose
11. Response to storefront; impression + later click events logged (anonymized) for CTR analytics
ai_search_query.target_latency: p95 <= 4 s end-to-end; hard timeout 6 s triggers classic-search fallback

data_flows.show_more: Storefront sends session cursor -> orchestrator excludes already-shown ids -> steps 7-11 rerun -> next 3-5 distinct suggestions
data_flows.classic_search_and_catalog: Storefront -> Search Proxy Lambda -> OpenSearch: completion suggester (as-you-type), fuzzy multi-field match (typos), filters + aggregations (facet counts), sort options; no LLM involved — always available
data_flows.catalog_sync: Saleor webhook -> SQS -> Indexer Lambda -> Saleor GraphQL fetch -> embed (if content changed) -> OpenSearch upsert; nightly reconciliation heals drift
data_flows.admin_change: Admin saves exclusion/rule in Saleor App -> DynamoDB write + OpenSearch flag update -> effective for all subsequent queries (cache entries for affected products invalidated)

off_topic_query_filtering.strategy: Three layers ordered by cost — cheapest checks run first so most junk never reaches the LLM

layers.name: Input constraints
layers.details: Max query length ~300 chars, single-turn only, WAF rate limits and per-visitor quotas upstream

layers.name: Retrieval-score gate
layers.details: Embed query and run OpenSearch hybrid search BEFORE any LLM call; if top-hit normalized score is below a threshold (tuned on the offline eval set), the query is off-topic or a genuine no-match -> return the honest 'not in our offer' path without invoking Claude. Primary cost saver: embedding is ~1000x cheaper than a generation

layers.name: Guardrails + in-call classification
layers.details: Bedrock Guardrails (Standard tier, Polish-optimized) blocks abuse and prompt attacks; denied topics for never-engage categories (medical/legal advice, politics). For soft off-topic cases that pass the score gate, the Haiku prompt returns {"off_topic": true} when the request is not product discovery — no extra LLM call; orchestrator maps it to a polite Polish redirect. Off-topic events logged separately from unmatched demand

llm_response_caching.strategy: Three complementary layers; cache stores chosen product IDs + explanations only, never rendered card data

layers.name: Exact-match cache
layers.details: Query normalized (lowercase, trim, strip diacritics/punctuation) then hashed as key. At serve time, product IDs are re-fetched from OpenSearch (single mget) and re-checked against hard filters — price changes, suspensions, and exclusions invalidate automatically

layers.name: Semantic cache
layers.details: Cached query embeddings stored with pointer to response; new query with cosine similarity >= ~0.95 reuses the cached result (e.g. 'czerwona sukienka na wesele' ~= 'sukienka czerwona wesele'). Main source of hit rate in free-text search

layers.name: Bedrock prompt caching
layers.details: Static system prompt marked as cache prefix; cache reads ~90% cheaper than regular input tokens. Candidates differ per query so only the prefix benefits — low impact, zero risk, config-level change

invalidation_rules: Serve-time revalidation: cached product IDs re-checked against current availability/exclusion filters; stale IDs dropped
Global cache-version key bumped on any admin edit to exclusions or manual rules — instantly invalidates all entries
'Show more' responses are not cached (session cursor makes them user-specific); alternatively include shown-IDs hash in the key
TTL bounds staleness even without events

requirements_traceability.requirement: AI search in hero, 3-5 suggestions with explanations
Show more suggestions -> further distinct set

covered_by: Retrieval boost + prompt rule + post-validation; badge flag indexed and rendered on all product cards

requirements_traceability.requirement: Honest handling of unavailable products, no invention, demand insight
Never suggest suspended partners / seasonally unavailable products
covered_by: Hard filter clause in every OpenSearch query + sync pipeline flags (target propagation < 5 min)
Client timeout budget, kill-switch feature flag, classic search is LLM-independent

requirements_traceability.requirement: Classic search: as-you-type, typo tolerance, name/partner/category/location/tags
covered_by: OpenSearch completion suggester, fuzziness, multi-field mapping with Polish analyzer

requirements_traceability.requirement: Catalog filters with result counts + sorting
Admin exclusion list + manual rules, effective immediately
covered_by: Admin Curation Saleor App -> DynamoDB + index flag + cache invalidation

requirements_traceability.requirement: Admin review: anonymized queries, CTR, unmatched requests
covered_by: Firehose -> S3 -> Athena dashboard in Saleor App

requirements_traceability.requirement: Usage limits to keep costs predictable
covered_by: WAF rate limits, per-visitor quotas, query cache, Guardrails, budget alarms

security_and_compliance.data_residency: All AWS resources in eu-central-1; Bedrock EU-geo inference profiles route only within EU regions; Bedrock does not use customer data for model training
security_and_compliance.gdpr: Search queries stored anonymized (no account linkage; hashed rotating visitor ID); 12-month retention with documented purpose (demand analysis); DPA with AWS in place via standard terms
security_and_compliance.secrets: Saleor app token and API keys in AWS Secrets Manager / Vercel encrypted env vars; storefront never holds AWS credentials
security_and_compliance.prompt_injection: Guardrails prompt-attack filter + strict system prompt + structured-output validation + candidate-set grounding limit blast radius to wrong-but-real product suggestions
security_and_compliance.waf: AWS WAF managed rules + rate-based rules on the AI endpoint

cost_projection_12_months.disclaimer: Indicative estimates in USD, based on AWS list prices checked via AWS documentation on 2026-07-06 where available; unit prices (especially Bedrock EU-region and Guardrails per-text-unit rates) must be re-verified in the AWS Pricing Calculator before contract commitment. Excludes Saleor Cloud and Vercel subscriptions (separate line items) and one-off development effort.

traffic_assumptions.month_1_3: 60k visits/mo, 8% try AI search, 1.6 requests/session avg -> ~8k AI generations/mo
traffic_assumptions.cache_hit_rate: 20% of queries served from cache (rising over time), already netted out of the generation counts above

unit_cost_model_per_ai_query.llm_claude_haiku_4_5: ~2,500 input tokens (prompt + 25 candidates) x $1.00/M + ~400 output tokens x $5.00/M = ~$0.0045
unit_cost_model_per_ai_query.guardrails_standard: ~2-3 text units per query, estimated ~$0.001-0.002 (verify rate)
unit_cost_model_per_ai_query.total_estimate: ~$0.006-0.008 per AI query; planning figure $0.01 gives ~30% headroom

monthly_costs_usd.item: Bedrock embeddings — indexing (initial ~50k products then deltas)
OpenSearch provisioned domain (3 nodes Multi-AZ + storage; scale up in m9)
Lambda + API Gateway + SQS + Step Functions

twelve_month_total_usd.recommended_annual_aws_budget: 10,000 USD with AWS Budgets alarm at 80%

cost_controls: Per-visitor daily quota and WAF rate limiting cap worst-case Bedrock spend
Normalized-query cache removes repeat-query LLM cost
Content-hash check avoids re-embedding unchanged products
Haiku-class model chosen over Sonnet-class (~3x cheaper) — quality gate via offline eval harness
Daily Bedrock spend alarm + automatic kill switch to classic search if budget breached
If sustained volume grows 5x beyond plan: evaluate Bedrock Flex/batch tiers and OpenSearch reserved instances

answers_to_epic_open_questions.question: Lowest ongoing operating/maintenance cost approach + 12-month projection
answers_to_epic_open_questions.answer: Serverless RAG on Bedrock (Haiku-class model, pay-per-token, no GPU or model ops) + one shared OpenSearch cluster for all search surfaces. Projection above: ~$7.5k base / $10k budgeted for year one, dominated by OpenSearch baseline and Bedrock per-query cost, both individually capped.

answers_to_epic_open_questions.question: Minimum product information for accurate suggestions
answers_to_epic_open_questions.answer: Required at offer onboarding: Polish name, >=300-character description, category, location, price, >=3 tags/attributes, photo. Enforce via Saleor attribute validation + indexer 'content completeness score'; products below threshold are indexed for classic search but down-weighted in AI retrieval; admin dashboard lists low-quality offers.

answers_to_epic_open_questions.question: English query on Polish site
answers_to_epic_open_questions.answer: MVP: detect language (Claude handles this in the same call), answer in Polish against the Polish catalog with a note the site is Polish-language; embeddings are multilingual so retrieval still works. Revisit language switching when the English storefront ships.

answers_to_epic_open_questions.question: 'Notify me when available' for unmatched requests
answers_to_epic_open_questions.answer: Not in MVP scope, but the unmatched-demand event schema includes an optional contact-consent field so the feature can be added without pipeline changes; follow-up owner should be the assortment/partnership team via the admin dashboard export.

answers_to_epic_open_questions.question: Two search entry points on one page
answers_to_epic_open_questions.answer: Yes, with distinct affordances: hero = conversational prompt ('Opisz, czego szukasz...'), header = compact classic search with autocomplete. AI results screen cross-links to classic results for the same query. Validate with usability testing in the design phase; A/B test hero variants post-launch.

answers_to_epic_open_questions.question: Success metrics for first months
answers_to_epic_open_questions.answer: Instrumented from day one: AI-search adoption (share of visitors), suggestion CTR, suggestion->product-page->cart conversion vs. classic-search baseline, zero-result/unmatched rate, cost per AI query, fallback activation rate. Targets to be agreed at kickoff; dashboard ships with MVP.

risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Quotas, cache, cheap model class, budget alarms with kill switch, cost-per-query on the admin dashboard (epic risk #1 addressed by design)
Candidate-set grounding makes fabrication structurally impossible; offline eval harness gates every change; admin exclusions and manual rules give day-one curation
Onboarding validation + completeness scoring + low-quality report for admins
Full funnel instrumentation and A/B capability from launch; success metrics reviewed monthly
Guardrails (Polish-optimized), WAF, per-visitor quotas, max input length, no free-form conversation (single-turn by design)
Model ID isolated in config; eval harness makes model swaps a one-week exercise; Bedrock offers multiple EU-available model families
SQS retries + DLQ + nightly full reconciliation; hard filters evaluated at query time against freshest index

implementation_phases.name: Foundations & spike
implementation_phases.scope: CDK infra skeleton, Saleor webhook -> OpenSearch pipeline, embedding model benchmark on Polish catalog sample (ADR-3 decision), classic search index with Polish analyzer

implementation_phases.name: Classic search & catalog
implementation_phases.scope: Search-as-you-type, typo tolerance, filters with counts, sorting, product cards with badge — shippable independently and serves as AI fallback

implementation_phases.name: AI suggestion engine
implementation_phases.scope: Orchestrator pipeline, Guardrails, prompt + structured output, show-more cursor, honest no-match path, quotas + cache + WAF, fallback wiring

implementation_phases.name: Admin app & analytics
implementation_phases.scope: Saleor App: exclusions, manual rules, dashboard (queries, CTR, unmatched); Firehose/Athena pipeline; anonymization

implementation_phases.name: Hardening & launch
implementation_phases.scope: Load test, offline quality eval sign-off, budget alarms, runbooks, pentest of AI endpoint, go-live with feature flag ramp-up

aws_services_used.purpose: Claude Haiku 4.5 (EU profile) for suggestion generation; Guardrails Standard tier for Polish moderation; embedding model for semantic retrieval
Hybrid vector + keyword index for AI retrieval, classic search, and catalog facets
Orchestrator, search proxy, indexer, analytics functions
Public API for storefront and Saleor webhooks; rate limiting and bot control
Quotas, query cache, exclusion list, manual rules, show-more cursors
Webhook buffering, scheduled reconciliation, sync orchestration
Anonymized event analytics for the admin dashboard

aws_services_used.service: CloudWatch, X-Ray, AWS Budgets, Secrets Manager
aws_services_used.purpose: Observability, tracing, cost alarms, secret storage
Infrastructure as code for the entire stack
